steps.title: Identify the relationship between methods and financial requirements
steps.content: The passage establishes that SAGE requires extensive financial support to perform longitudinal methods for causal analysis. Then it introduces a contrast: when causation is irrelevant, longitudinal methods become unnecessary. The key signal is this necessity chain — if a method isn't needed, neither is the cost that supports it.

steps.title: Apply this necessity relationship to the choices
steps.content: Because the sociology study doesn't need longitudinal methods, it requires significantly less financial outlay than SAGE. The other options either misidentify what's being compared, shift to a different criterion unrelated to necessity and cost, or simply restate what's already established. That matches choice B — B is correct.
